draft.title: weather app for runners
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
weather app for runners

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Runners need live pace data and weather side-by-side during runs, not sequential views
facade.axisTargeted: Information layout — parallel data streams vs. sequential screens
facade.label: Split screen: pace + weather
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors real-time dual-metric dashboards; runner attention splits between performance and conditions
facade.rejectImplies: Design favors single-metric focus or temporal switching; weather is background context, not co-equal with pace

evidence.hypothesis: Runners need live pace data and weather side-by-side during runs, not sequential views
evidence.implication: Design favors real-time dual-metric dashboards; runner attention splits between performance and conditions

facade.hypothesis: A weather app for runners works better with encouraging, conversational language than neutral reporting
facade.label: Casual, motivational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Use upbeat copy, emoji accents, energy-forward alerts (e.g. 'Perfect running weather!' vs 'Clear conditions')
facade.rejectImplies: Keep tone factual and minimal, let data speak for itself, remove personality from text

facade.hypothesis: Runners benefit from motion that feels effortless and breathing-like rather than jarring—transitions should drift in gradually, matching the rhythm of a long run
facade.label: Soft fade transitions
facade.acceptImplies: Motion design should prioritize ease and organic timing over instant feedback; weather info reveals smoothly as the runner navigates
facade.rejectImplies: Motion should be crisp and responsive; instant state changes with minimal animation; feedback is immediate, not gradual

facade.hypothesis: A clean, light aesthetic with high contrast and simple rectangular containers will feel approachable and fast—important for runners who want quick weather info without visual noise.
facade.label: Bright minimal cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers stripped-down, bright interfaces. Next probes should explore: flat design, sans-serif typography, generous whitespace, maybe pastel accents.
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer darker tones, more visual texture, or richer visual hierarchy. Probe towards: dark mode, photographic backgrounds, or layered depth next.

facade.hypothesis: For a runner's weather app, combining a persistent sidebar for quick stats (temp, pace alerts) with tabbed sections (forecast, route conditions, warnings) balances glance-ability against navigation depth without forcing full-page reloads.
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure: sidebar persistence vs tab-based switching
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs
facade.acceptImplies: User values quick access to key metrics alongside deeper browsing; lean toward hybrid layouts that keep critical info visible while allowing tab-based exploration of secondary content.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers either single-screen density (tabs only, no sidebar) or full-page views (sidebar only, no tabs); navigation should be one clear pattern, not two competing structures.

facade.hypothesis: For a running weather app, users might prefer clean, high-contrast design that's easy to read at a glance during outdoor activity — minimizing cognitive load and visual clutter.
facade.label: Bright, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: User leans toward sleek, modern, utilitarian design. Suggests preference for clarity and speed over personality or warmth.
facade.rejectImplies: User may want more personality, texture, or natural/organic visual language. Could indicate preference for approachability over pure efficiency.

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimalist, clean aesthetics vs. data-rich visual displays for workout tracking
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants quick-glance info at top vs. scrollable detailed forecast layers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational, encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, focused atmosphere vs. energetic, action-oriented feel
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prioritizes running-specific metrics (pace impact, humidity) vs. standard weather only
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers smooth, gradual transitions vs. snappy, instant updates and alerts
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Runners prefer raw metrics (pace, temp, humidity) without interpretive warnings or severity flags during active runs
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data vs. key metrics — whether to show unfiltered numbers or curated/contextualized data
facade.label: Numbers only, no alerts
facade.acceptImplies: Design strips prose and warnings; trusts runners to self-interpret conditions; favors minimalist metric display
facade.rejectImplies: Design layers advisory copy, alert states, or narrative interpretation (e.g., 'Caution: high heat') to guide decision-making in real-time

draft.title: RunWeather
draft.summary: A weather app designed for runners, showing real-time conditions, wind impact, and run-friendly forecasts at a glance.
draft.html:
RunWeather

San Francisco, CA

2:00 PM Sunny 65°F
4:00 PM Sunny 64°F
6:00 PM Clear 58°F
